hardRules: Experiments may not weaken, replace, or fabricate current conformance receipts.
No React runtime dependency in downstream output.
No component IDs or component-specific branches in target lowerers.
No copied final HTML, screenshot-to-code inference, gallery repair CSS, innerHTML/@html, or post-mount reconstruction.
One writer per exact path; maximum four parallel workers.
Every result is deterministic, content-addressed, benchmarked, and includes exact commands and failures.
A failed approach is recorded and retired rather than patched indefinitely.

decisions.solid: salvage lowerer scaffolding only: consumes synthetic IR and compiles, but ID-based tag branches violate genericity and states/classes/behavior are absent
decisions.svelte: salvage lowerer scaffolding only: consumes synthetic IR and compiles, but ignores states, bindings, classes, canonical topology and behavior
decisions.verification: retire: disconnected from generated code and trivially gameable by copied traces
decisions.vue: retire: violates no copied final HTML by rebuilding templates from trace.dom; state snapshots are copied evidence, not IR lowering

evidence: parts have empty conditions and bindings and generic token names
IR topology conflicts with observed trace parts (for example button IR label/spinner versus canonical root/control)

repairPlan: Replace fixture IR with validated frontend-derived IR carrying exact structure, bindings, classes, conditions, state transitions, and provenance; reject placeholder canonical paths.
Define a target-neutral semantics/tag model so lowerers do not branch on component or part IDs; add static AST checks for component and part-ID branching.
Generate native stateful source from IR only. Do not read canonical trace DOM or presentation to construct output.
Build each generated shard with its real Vue/Svelte/Solid compiler and mount it in isolated native harnesses for every contract state and viewport.
Recapture candidate DOM, accessibility, events, geometry, styles, and screenshots from those harnesses; bind receipts to generated-source/build/harness digests.
Keep canonical traces read-only and outside candidate output paths; reject byte-identical trace/screenshot reuse and add a copy-attack self-test that must fail.
Require exact topology, classes, states, behavior and zero-pixel-diff matrices for all 108 cells before passing.
Make verification self-check fail when the audited matrix fails; separately test deterministic failure receipts.

verifier.attackExplanation: copying each canonical tracer trace byte-for-byte into any searched target path makes every projected stage equal; verifier has no build, native runtime, framework identity, provenance separation, or screenshot recapture check
verifier.reason: candidate paths only accept trace.json files, while lowerers emit framework source and no browser traces
verifier.selfCheckExplanation: self-check exits zero when the receipt status is failed and only requires missing-output diagnostics
